data.title: Private credit valuations under pressure; back to fundamentals for buyout firms in 2026: MSCI
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] There are signs of more pressure on private credit loans in 2026, as higher base rates challenged borrowersГўВҖВҷ ability to fulfil interest burdens, said data platform MSCI.

What began as a trickle of write-downs in 2022 has become a larger leak, as senior loans or loans that take top repayment priority saw write-downs of 20 per cent have tripled since 2022. The loan values for 11 per cent of mezzanine loans have been cut in half according to data from MSCI Private Capital Universe.

Widespread restructuring to mop up troubled loans have not yet occurred, and repricings have increased to patch things with loan amendments. Deeper impairments are creeping up the capital structure as 5 per cent of senior loans have experienced more than 50 per cent write-downs.

ГўВҖВңThese more aggressive markdowns suggest an increasing number of loans are circling the drain, sliding toward restructuring ГўВҖВ“ the point where debt holders risk becoming equity holders,ГўВҖВқ said MSCI.

Loans appear to be generating income to compensate credit losses, but news of bankruptcies appears to be growing. Declining interest rates may give some relief to struggling borrowers to repair balance sheets and allow loan valuations to recover, it said.

But persistently high inflation slows the pace of rate cuts, which will require more drastic adjustments in marks to market. The scattered signs of stress may emerge as a pattern that will test the resilience of private creditГўВҖВҷs recent expansion, it added.

The relative youth of private credit as a major investment class means that it has yet to undergo a full cycle. There is still an unknown on how the asset class will perform as an increasing volume of borrowers struggle to pay back loans, it said.

ГўВҖВңSigns of borrower stress after two years of elevated base rates have already emerged; whether a more settled economic outlook and a hoped-for downward path in central bank rates eases that pressure is yet to be seen,ГўВҖВқ said MSCI.

Value bridge

For buyout firms, value creation between acquisition and exits ГўВҖВ“ known as the value bridge ГўВҖВ“ has been increasingly driven by revenue growth, it said. Revenue growth has accounted for just under two-thirds of incremental value generated above invested capital for buyouts that exited between 2022 to the third quarter of 2025. Multiple expansion is contributing less significantly and margin improvement is adding a smaller uplift than previously.

This is a break from the past when over half the incremental gains came from earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation-multiple expansion. This underscores the elevated exit-valuation multiples at the peak of the low-rate cycle, said MSCI.

The picture was more balanced between 2015 and 2019, with incremental gains split almost evenly between revenue growth and multiple expansion. Margin growthГўВҖВҷs role was a minor contributor to value creation in the 2015to 2019 exits. This role is now increasing, indicating an emphasis on profitability, added MSCI.

This has implications for liquidity and has seen improvements in fund-level distribution rates track portfolio company margins. This reinforces the idea that cash back to limited partners is tied to portfolio companiesГўВҖВҷ fundamentals, it said.

On the valuation front, revenue is doing the heavy lifting in the value bridge, and any slowdown in top-line growth could threaten potential exit valuation and distribution, it added.

ГўВҖВңIn short, in 2026 limited partners and general partners may find that crossing the buyout value bridge depends far less on market sentiment, and far more on whether portfolio companies can grow and sustain their fundamentals,ГўВҖВқ said MSCI.

data.title: UOB jumps 2.3% to 9-month high as market rises; Macquarie upgrades stock
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] Shares of UOB rose on Thursday (Jan 22) after Macquarie upgraded its call on the lender and raised its target price.

The counter climbed 2.3 per cent or S$0.83 to S$37.60 in early trade and hit a nine-month high. The last time UOB shares traded higher was in April 2025, ShareInvestor data showed.

As at 3.18pm, the counter was trading at S$37.51, still up by 2 per cent, with close to four million shares changing hands.

In a Wednesday note, Macquarie analyst Jayden Vantarakis upgraded UOB to an ГўВҖВңoutperformГўВҖВқ rating and lifted its target price to S$41, 11.5 per cent above its latest closing price of S$36.77.

Vantarakis sees a potential ГўВҖВңrelative-value catch up playГўВҖВқ for UOB as headwinds are expected to abate this earnings season.

ГўВҖВңSeasonal trends are a headwind for non-interest income. Lower rates are a double-edged sword, pressuring revenues in FY2026 but benefiting valuations,ГўВҖВқ he said.

Vantarakis highlighted that loan volumes have risen, as the latest banking statistics from the Monetary Authority of Singapore in November reflect a ГўВҖВңsustained pickup in lendingГўВҖВқ. This comes as interest rates are expected to continue trending downwards on Singapore dollar strength and US dollar weakness.

ГўВҖВңWhilst we expect some slowdown (for loan volume) in FY2026 to reflect front-loading impacts for manufacturing and exports, this sets a positive base for (the yearГўВҖВҷs estimated) loan momentum,ГўВҖВқ he said.

Vantakaris forecasts UOBГўВҖВҷs quarterly provisions normalising after taking large one-off provisions in the third quarter, given that the bank has topped up allowances and as rate movements should be ГўВҖВңmarginally supportive of collateral valuesГўВҖВқ.

Macquarie noted that quarter-on-quarter momentum for Q4 FY2025ГўВҖВҷs estimated profit after tax and minority interests could be affected by how Singapore banks have recorded lower trading and other non-interest income over the past five years, but UOB could benefit from a lower base in Q3 FY2025.

While major US banks recently reported a quarter-on-quarter decline in overall wealth assets by 2 per cent, Singapore banks are expected to do better on wealth asset under management inflows, he added, given that the relative ГўВҖВңsafe-havenГўВҖВқ status of the city-stateГўВҖВҷs banking sector.

However, earnings growth is expected to remain lacklustre in FY2026, which may present headwinds, Vantakaris said.

UOB will be releasing its full-year financial results for FY2025 on Feb 24.

data.title: Malaysia's central bank holds key rate at 2.75%
data.description:
[KUALA LUMPUR] MalaysiaГўВҖВҷs central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at its first policy review of the year on Thursday (Jan 22), as expected by economists, amid steady economic growth and modest inflation.

Bank Negara Malaysia kept its overnight policy rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent, as had been predicted by all 30 economists surveyed in a Reuters poll.

The central bank last lowered its main policy rate in July 2025 in a preemptive move after US President Donald Trump imposed steep tariffs on most trading partners.

Since then, trade-related uncertainty has eased and the tariff rate on Malaysia has been reduced to 19 per cent from 25 per cent, broadly in line with other Asian peers.

ГўВҖВңFor 2026, while the impact of tariffs could weigh on global growth, the outlook remains resilient, supported by sustained domestic demand, moderating inflation, robust tech investments, and supportive fiscal and monetary policies,ГўВҖВқ BNM said in a statement.

The central bank said the current key interest rate level is ГўВҖВңappropriate and supportive of the economy amid price stabilityГўВҖВқ.

MalaysiaГўВҖВҷs economy expanded 5.7 per cent in the final quarter of 2025, accelerating from the 5.2 per cent growth recorded in the previous three months, preliminary government data showed last week.

That brought 2025 full-year growth to 4.9 per cent, just below the 5.1 per cent expansion recorded in 2024 and surpassing official projections of between 4 per cent and 4.8 per cent, the data showed.

Official data has not yet been finalised, but BNM said economic growth in 2025 is expected to be at the upper end of the forecast range, adding that the momentum is expected to continue this year, supported by resilient domestic demand.

The government and central bank expects the economy to grow between 4 per cent and 4.5 per cent this year, amid lingering uncertainties arising from the impact of US tariffs.

Inflation has remained moderate with the consumer price index rising 1.6 per cent in December from a year earlier, accelerating slightly from a 1.4 per cent increase the previous month.

Headline and core inflation averaged 1.4 per cent and 2.0 per cent, respectively, in 2025, the central bank said.

ГўВҖВңFor 2026, headline inflation is expected to remain moderate amid the continued easing in global cost conditions,ГўВҖВқ it said, adding that core inflation this year will remain stable. REUTERS

data.title: Nvidia CEO says AI will create jobs for electricians and plumbers that pay six figures
data.description:
AS ARTIFICIAL intelligence (AI) threatens to upends job markets in countries around the world, Nvidia chief executive officer Jensen Huang brushed off longer-term concerns and made the case that skilled vocational workers are seeing increasing demand now.

Plumbers, electricians and construction workers are going to be able to command ГўВҖВңsix-figure salariesГўВҖВқ, thanks to demand to build data centers that run and train AI, he said in an interview with BlackRock CEO Larry Fink at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland on Wednesday (Jan 21).

The technology will require one of the biggest infrastructure buildouts in history, with trillions of dollars in new investment, Huang said.

ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷre seeing quite a significant boom in this area. Salaries have gone up nearly double,ГўВҖВқ Huang said. ГўВҖВңEverybody should be able to make a great living. You donГўВҖВҷt need to have a PhD in computer science to do so.ГўВҖВқ

HuangГўВҖВҷs comments echo remarks made at Davos on Tuesday by Palantir Technologies CEO Alex Karp, who praised workers with ГўВҖВңvocational trainingГўВҖВқ and said AI would create more local jobs and largely eliminate the need for mass immigration.

CoreweaveГўВҖВҷs Michael Intrator also touched on the ГўВҖВңphysicalityГўВҖВқ of the AI boom at a panel later on Wednesday, with the data center firmГўВҖВҷs CEO describing the need for growing numbers of plumbers, electricians and carpenters.ГӮВ 

Nvidia, the leading maker of chips that help power and run the latest AI models, has benefited from the huge rush to build data centers. The company is on track to generate almost US$200 billion in data center chip sales for 2025, according to an average analyst estimate compiled by Bloomberg.

To date, the bulk of its revenue comes from the biggest data center builders ГўВҖВ“ Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Amazon.com and Alphabet ГўВҖВ“ but itГўВҖВҷs striking deals with a growing number of smaller data center operators. Tech firms have committed to spend a combined US$500 billion in data center leases in the coming years.

AIГўВҖВҷs impact on the job market is already being felt. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has warned about a ГўВҖВңwhite-collar bloodbathГўВҖВқ that could wipe out 50 per cent of entry-level jobs. The companyГўВҖВҷs Claude AI is gaining attention for its coding abilities, a feature that could displace more junior programmers.ГӮВ 

ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷre entering a world where the junior-level software engineers ГўВҖВ“ maybe many of the tasks of the more senior-level software engineers ГўВҖВ“ are starting to be done most of the way by AI systems. Now thatГўВҖВҷs going to go further,ГўВҖВқ Amodei said.

While he believes the good from the technology will outweigh the bad, high unemployment and underemployment are risks that need to be mitigated.ГӮВ 

ГўВҖВңThereГўВҖВҷs going to be unfortunately a whole class of people who are, across a lot of industries, going to have a hard time coping,ГўВҖВқ he said.ГӮВ 

On Wednesday, Fink noticeably avoided probing Huang on sensitive topics, most notably China. NvidiaГўВҖВҷs sales to the country are controversial, and the company is waiting to hear whether it will be able to sell its chips to the country and in what quantities. On Tuesday, Amodei called shipping Nvidia chips into China similar to selling nuclear weapons to North Korea.ГӮВ 

Huang is expected to visit China at the end of this month as he works to reopen a crucial market for the companyГўВҖВҷs AI chips.

It is a pivotal time for the business, after the US moved to ease limits on chip exports to China itГўВҖВҷs had in place since 2022. Nvidia is still blocked from shipping the top-of-the-line chips to the country, stymieing BeijingГўВҖВҷs ability to innovate beyond the cutting-edge of AI, but will be able to ship older-generation H200 AI chips.

China, meanwhile, is expected to clear H200 imports for commercial use in the first three months of 2026, though it wonГўВҖВҷt allow the chips for use in the military, sensitive government agencies, critical infrastructure or its state-owned enterprises, Bloomberg News reported.

Some of the countryГўВҖВҷs most prominent tech firms, including Alibaba and ByteDance, have privately expressed interest in ordering than 200,000 units each of the chip.ГӮВ BLOOMBERG

data.title: Singapore users are flocking to Elon MuskГўВҖВҷs Grok despite deepfake controversy
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] A wave of controversy over sexually explicit artificial intelligence (AI) deepfakes has not made billionaire Elon MuskГўВҖВҷs social media platform X or chatbot Grok any less popular in Singapore.

Instead, it may have done the opposite.

Since late December 2025, X has been at the centre of a storm over users creating AI-generated sexually explicit images of other users without their consent. Users could do so simply by posting a message aimed at GrokГўВҖВҷs account on the social media platform.

In the wake of such controversy, the Grok app has shot up the charts on the Google and Apple app stores for Singapore users, according to data from analytics firm SensorTower.

On Google Play StoreГўВҖВҷs top free apps chart, Grok has been in the top 10 for most of January ГўВҖВ“ a feat it last achieved in late November.

On the Apple App Store top free apps chart, Grok has hovered in the top 25 since early January, up from around the No100 to 130 range in October.ГӮВ 

X continues to hover in the top 100 apps in both stores, with interest rising in the Google Play Store in January after a late December slump.

On Jan 15, X announced that it had implemented measures to prevent the Grok account on X from editing images of real people. However, such image generation features remain available on the standalone Grok app and website.

Among the most headline-grabbing incidents was American conservative influencer Ashley St Clair ГўВҖВ“ who is mother to one of MuskГўВҖВҷs children ГўВҖВ“ filing a lawsuit against xAI, the company that created Grok, on Jan 15. The lawsuit alleged that the company allowed users to create deepfakes of her in sexually explicit poses, including images of when she was a minor.

Indonesia and Malaysia became the first countries in the world to ban Grok over AI deepfakes earlier in January. SingaporeГӮВ is currently engaging with X over the generation and distribution of non-consensual intimate images, according to the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA).

Under SingaporeГўВҖВҷs Code of Practice for Online Safety, X is a designated social media service that is required to curb the spread of harmful and inappropriate content on its platform, IMDA told The Straits Times on Jan 20.

Ng Wi En, a PhD researcher at Nanyang Technological University studying technology-facilitated sexual violence, noted that the gender-based harm enabled by these apps often exists in a context that reframes them as entertainment or social commentary.

As it takes place digitally and anonymously, the harm caused by deepfakes can feel distant to perpetrators, she added. Coupled with the scale at which it occurs, such harm has been normalised, allowing demand to persist until public scrutiny or institutional intervention forces a response.

ГўВҖВңAn outright ban may appear decisive, but on its own is unlikely to produce lasting change,ГўВҖВқ said Dr Michelle Ho, an assistant professor at the National University of SingaporeГўВҖВҷs department of communications and new media.

ГўВҖВңSexually explicit deepfakes are enabled by cultural assumptions about gender, consent and whose harm counts,ГўВҖВқ said Dr Ho, who noted that sustainedГӮВ public education and institutional engagementГӮВ is necessary to shift how these technologies are understood and used today.

ГўВҖВңThis includes recognising digitally mediated sexual harm as a form of gender-based violence rather than a fringe misuse of AI,ГўВҖВқ she added.

X and Grok drew more than 13 million and 14 million app downloads worldwide respectively in December, according to SensorTower. X and Grok were also the seventh and 59th most visited websites in Singapore in December, according to data from analytics firm Similarweb.

Musk acquired X, formerly known as Twitter, in 2022 and founded xAI in 2023.

AI-generated deepfakes have been a persistent issue on X since as far back as January 2024, when the platform temporarily banned searches for American pop star Taylor Swift after users shared sexually explicit deepfake images of the celebrity.

Among GrokГўВҖВҷs differentiating features from its larger chatbot rivals are its irreverent tone and focus on adult content. Musk often promotes GrokГўВҖВҷs companion feature for paying users, which includes an anime-style character that can engage in flirtatious conversations and change into suggestive outfits. THE STRAITS TIMES

data.title: China, Indonesia, Malaysia top Changi Airport markets in 2025; total passenger traffic hits record
data.description:
[SINGAPORE]ГӮВ China, Indonesia and Malaysia were Changi AirportГўВҖВҷs top passenger markets in 2025, the airport operator said on Thursday (Jan 22), amid a record 69.98 million passengers passing through its terminals over the year.

The figure eclipsed the previous high of 68.3 million passengers recorded in 2019, before the Covid-19 pandemic grounded planes and disrupted global air travel.

Passenger traffic in 2025 was also 3.4 per cent higher than in 2024, when Changi handled 67.7 million passengers.

December 2025 was the busiest month of the year, with 6.3 million passenger movements, according to statistics released by Changi Airport Group (CAG).

The busiest day was Dec 20 ГўВҖВ“ the Saturday before Christmas ГўВҖВ“ when more than 223,000 passengers passed through the airport.

China was ChangiГўВҖВҷs largest passenger market for the year, followed by Indonesia, Malaysia, Australia, India and Thailand, CAG said. China also recorded the strongest growth, with passenger movements rising 12.2 per cent compared with 2024. Vietnam and Japan were among the fastest-growing markets,ГӮВ posting year-on-year increases of 9.8 per cent and 7 per cent respectively.

The busiest routes in 2025 were Kuala Lumpur, Bangkok, Jakarta, Bali (Denpasar) and Hong Kong.

Changi Airport handled 374,000 aircraft movements ГўВҖВ“ including landings and take-offs ГўВҖВ“ in 2025, up 2.2 per cent from a year earlier.

The airportГўВҖВҷs network also expanded at a record pace. CAG said 13 new city links were added in 2025, marking its strongest year for network growth.

The new destinations were Changchun, Harbin, Lanzhou, Yichang and Zhangjiajie in China; Labuan Bajo, Padang and Semarang in Indonesia; Vienna in Austria; Vijayawada in India; Kota Bharu in Malaysia; Ulaanbaatar in Mongolia; and Nha Trang in Vietnam.

Changi also welcomed two new passenger airlines: MIAT Mongolian Airlines and Jakarta-based Pelita Air.

CAG chief executive Yam Kum Weng said Changi had a strong year, driven by record passenger traffic, network expansion and solid air cargo performance despite global volatility.

Looking ahead, he said the airport would expand its network in Asia further, particularly into emerging secondary cities, while improving efficiency and the traveller experience.

ГўВҖВңOur goal is to ensure that Changi stays at the forefront of aviation, offering travellers exceptional airport experiences while serving as a key gateway to the region,ГўВҖВқ Mr Yam said.

As at January 2026, about 100 airlines operate more than 7,300 scheduled flights a week at Changi, connecting Singapore to over 170 cities in 50 countries and territories worldwide.

Its target is to have more than 200 city links by the mid-2030s, when the mega Terminal 5 opens.

T5 will raise the airportГўВҖВҷs yearly passenger capacity by more than 55 per cent, from the current 90 million to 140 million.

Changi handled 2.08 million tonnes of airfreight in 2025, up 4.5 per cent from the year before, making it one of the airportГўВҖВҷs strongest cargo performances on record.

The top five air cargo markets were China, the United States, Australia, Hong Kong and India, CAG said. THE STRAITS TIMES

data.title: CGSI initiates coverage on Centurion Accomodation Reit with ГўВҖВҳaddГўВҖВҷ, S$1.38 target price
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] Analysts are turning bullish on the Centurion ecosystem, with CGS International (CGSI) initiating coverage on the newly listed Centurion Accommodation Reit (CAReit) while RHB and UOB Kay Hian see upside in its sponsor, Centurion Corporation.

CGSI initiated coverage on CAReit on Jan 21 with an ГўВҖВңaddГўВҖВқ rating and a target price of S$1.38, representing a potential upside from its trading price of S$1.10 at the time of CGSIГўВҖВҷs report.

CAReit was listed on the SGX mainboard in September last year, with an initial public offering (IPO) price of S$0.88 per unit.

CGSI Analysts Li Jialin and Lock Mun Yee describe the counter as offering ГўВҖВңhidden valueГўВҖВқ and being ГўВҖВңprimed for growthГўВҖВқ.

They project FY2026 and FY2027 distribution per unit (DPU) growth of 27.4 per cent and 10.1 per cent respectively, driven by operational bed capacity exceeding IPO forecasts.

This growth is attributed to the accelerated addition of 1,980 beds at Westlite Mandai, expected to be operational by April 2026, and the retention of 664 beds at Westlite Toh Guan until late 2028.ГӮВ 

ГўВҖВңWe like CAReit for its strong and visible DPU growth and potential for inorganic and organic opportunities,ГўВҖВқ the analysts said, noting that the Reit offers a FY2026 forecast dividend yield of 6.5 per cent.

ГўВҖВңMulti-year sector tailwinds, such as the construction upcycle in Singapore and robust demand for (purpose built student accommodation) in UK and Australia, are likely to underpin CAReitГўВҖВҷs robust earnings outlook,ГўВҖВқ the analysts said.ГӮВ 

Parent company rerates

Meanwhile, RHB has maintained ГўВҖВңbuyГўВҖВқ on Centurion Corporation, and raised its target price to S$1.88 from S$1.86 on Jan 21.

ГўВҖВңWe remain positive on Centurion CorpГўВҖВҷs prospects, as the group focuses on property development, acquisitions, and being CAReitГўВҖВҷs manager post spin-off of its assets to the latter,ГўВҖВқ analyst Alfie Yeo said.ГӮВ 

The target price upgrade follows the completion of the divestment of Epiisod Macquarie Park to CAReit for approximately A$345 million (S$280 million).

RHB increased its target price based on a higher sum-of-the-parts valuation, reflecting the slightly higher market valuation of its stake in CAReit. The brokerage also highlights potential future rewards for shareholders, noting that Centurion may declare another dividend in specie as it pares down its stake in the Reit next year.

Centurion Corporation shares were trading at S$1.41 at the time of RHBГўВҖВҷs report.

Separately, UOB Kay Hian named Centurion Corporation a top pick in its construction sector update on Jan 20, with a target price of S$1.90.

The brokerage maintains an ГўВҖВңoverweightГўВҖВқ call on the construction sector, and notes that the industry is ГўВҖВңset to outperform, with strong construction demand and order book visibility of two to four yearsГўВҖВқ.

Since its previous construction sector note, ГўВҖВңthe sector has undergone a meaningful valuation reratingГўВҖВқ, the brokerage said. This is driven by a construction upcycle supported by post-pandemic project catch-up and a new wave of infrastructure investments.

data.title: Knot most likely heir to Lagarde at ECB
data.description:
[BRUSSELS] Dutchman Klaas Knot is most likely to succeed Christine Lagarde at the helm of the European Central Bank, despite other candidates being better qualified, a Bloomberg survey of economists suggests.

Knot, whose stint as head of the NetherlandsГўВҖВҷ central bank ended last year, leads a ranking that places Bank for International Settlements chief Pablo Hernandez de Cos second and Bundesbank president Joachim Nagel third.

At the same time, respondents singled out executive board member Isabel Schnabel as having the best skill set to take over from Lagarde when her term expires next year. De Cos, a former governor of the Bank of Spain, was second with Knot close behind.

The poll comes after CroatiaГўВҖВҷs Boris Vujcic got the nod this week to become ECB vice president from June ГўВҖВ“ the first step in a two-year reshuffle of the Frankfurt-based institutionГўВҖВҷs executive board. ГӮВ 

Hailing from the regionГўВҖВҷs east, VujcicГўВҖВҷs appointment leaves the door open to candidates from both the north and south to compete for LagardeГўВҖВҷs position. Similarly, his moderate hawkishness is unlikely to be a major issue as eurozone officials seek to strike a balance between the profiles of the ECBГўВҖВҷs two top policymakers.

Whoever prevails will inherit an institution facing a far more unpredictable global backdrop, with recent weeks underscoring how quickly geopolitical and institutional norms can be tested.

Survey respondents value an economics degree and work experience within a central bank as most important for an ECB president. A career in finance and expertise running a monetary authority are also seen as useful, as are stints in politics and other European institutions.

Being a woman ГўВҖВ“ a flashpoint in past leadership selections ГўВҖВ“ is among the least important considerations, according to the poll.

Rumours about front-runners have circulated for months, focusing mainly on Knot and de Cos. Both are highly regarded and have been praised publicly by Lagarde herself.

Knot ГўВҖВңhas the intellect, he has the stamina, he is capable of including people and that is a skill that is rare and is very necessary,ГўВҖВқ she said in October. SheГўВҖВҷs described de Cos as a ГўВҖВңfantastic team playerГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңvery thorough economist.ГўВҖВқГӮВ 

Knot was one of the ECBГўВҖВҷs most influential officials during his 14 years on the governing council, eventually staking out more middle-ground positions after starting his career on a hawkish foot. He also helped keep global regulatory coordination intact through periods of market stress as chairman of the Financial Stability Board.

Schnabel, too, has been encouraged by Lagarde, who said last month there are ГўВҖВңplenty of very good candidates, and Isabel is one of them.ГўВҖВқ An economics professor and an adviser to GermanyГўВҖВҷs government before joining the ECB in 2020, she digs deeper into key debates than many peers and has become the official economists listen to most.

Schnabel, despite being viewed as the most capable candidate, faces legal hurdles, however, since ECB appointments are non-renewable and thereГўВҖВҷs no clear evidence yet of a loophole. Her policy stance ГўВҖВ“ at the very hawkish end of the spectrum ГўВҖВ“ could also be an impediment.

In general, ГўВҖВңyou probably want someone more centrist to maintain the consensus-building nature and to placate the sort of polarised elements of the Governing Council,ГўВҖВқ said Andrzej Szczepaniak, a former ECB economist who now works at Nomura.ГӮВ 

While Knot, de Cos and Nagel would fit that bill, their governments are yet to reveal their plans at this early stage.ГӮВ 

The Netherlands and Germany havenГўВҖВҷt commented publicly, though Lars-Hendrik Roeller ГўВҖВ“ a former economic adviser to Chancellor Angela Merkel ГўВҖВ“ has said itГўВҖВҷs time for one of his countrymen to get the presidency.

Spain does want another Executive Board seat once incumbent Vice President Luis de Guindos leaves in May, according to Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo.

Other candidates deemed likely or qualified successors to Lagarde include FranceГўВҖВҷs Francois Villeroy de Galhau and ItalyГўВҖВҷs Fabio Panetta, whose countries have both held the presidency at least once. ThereГўВҖВҷs also FinlandГўВҖВҷs Olli Rehn, who came second in the vice-presidency race.ГӮВ 

Among less likely options for survey resondents are European Investment Bank president Nadia Calvino from Spain and Joerg Kukies, GermanyГўВҖВҷs interim finance minister after its last government collapsed.

Amid the speculation, itГўВҖВҷs worth remembering that a dark-horse candidate could yet emerge, with Lagarde herself not among the favourites before being nominated.ГӮВ 

Most respondents expect a decision in the second quarter of 2027, though some see an earlier deal. Almost half thinks it will be part of a broader package that includes replacing chief economist Philip Lane, with another quarter anticipating a three-way agreement covering SchnabelГўВҖВҷs seat as well. BLOOMBERG

data.title: Rise in Singapore industrial rents tapers to 2.4% for 2025 after almost flat Q4: JTCГӮ
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] Rents of SingaporeГўВҖВҷs industrial spaces rose 2.4 per cent in 2025, slowing from a 3.5 per cent gain in 2024 and an 8.9 per cent surge in 2023.

The rise in the JTC rental index for all industrial space marked its smallest annual increase since 2021, based on data released on Thursday (Jan 22).ГӮВ 

On a quarterly basis, industrial rents edged up 0.5 per cent in the fourth quarter. While momentum is slowing, the Q4 data nevertheless showed 21 straight quarters of increases from Q4 2020.

Rents in the multiple-user factory segment inched up 0.2 per cent in Q4, increasing 1.8 per cent year on year.ГӮВ 

Rents for warehouses grew 1.1 per cent quarter on quarter and were up 3 per cent from Q4 2024. In the single-user factory segment, rents increased 0.7 per cent on a quarterly basis and 2.7 per cent on a yearly basis. Business park rents grew 0.4 per cent from Q3 and were up 2.6 per cent over the year.

In Q4, the price index of all industrial spaces gained 1.4 per cent from Q3. All industrial prices were up 5 per cent from the year-ago period.ГӮВ 

Multiple-user factory prices rose 1.9 per cent in Q4, while single-user factory prices were down 0.3 per cent from Q3. On a yearly basis, prices of multiple-user factories climbed 5.8 per cent, while prices of single-user factories increased 2.6 per cent.ГӮВ 

Overall occupancy stood at 88.7 per cent, down 0.4 percentage point from 89.1 per cent in the previous quarter and 0.3 percentage point lower than the 89 per cent recorded a year earlier.ГӮВ 

The decline was driven by strong completions, leading to an increase in total industrial stock by 345,000 square metres (sq m), JTC said.ГӮВ 

The warehouse and business park segments logged marginally higher occupancies compared with the previous quarter. In Q4, warehouses had an occupancy of 89.8 per cent, from 89.6 per cent, while business parks had an occupancy of 77.1 per cent, from 77 per cent in Q3.ГӮВ 

Occupancy for the multiple-user and single-user factory segments declined slightly in Q4.

The occupancy rate for the multiple-user factory segment stood at 89.9 per cent in the fourth quarter, down from 91 per cent in the third quarter. The single-user factory segment recorded an occupancy of 88.8 per cent, declining from 89.1 per cent previously.ГӮВ 

Total available stock in Q4 stood at 54.3 million sq m, increasing from the 53.9 million sq m available in the previous quarter.ГӮВ 

Transaction sale volume, according to estimates based on caveats lodged for industrial properties, fell 3 per cent in 2025 compared to 2024. Meanwhile, rental transaction volume rose 2 per cent in 2025 from 2024.ГӮВ 

During the quarter, JTC allocated a total of 67,800 sq m of ready-built facilities (RBF) to industrialists. This included 33,700 sq m of high-rise space and 29,300 sq m of land-based factory space.ГӮВ 

The total amount of RBF space returned in Q4 amounted to 103,900 sq m, of which 47,700 sq m was high-rise space and 37,300 sq m was land-based factory space.ГӮВ ГӮВ 

Among the high-rise space that was allocated, about 39 per cent was in JTCГўВҖВҷs newer developments which include JTC Defu Industrial City, Bulim Square and 1 North Coast.ГӮВ 

In the coming year, JTC expects one million sq m of industrial space to be completed.

Some 53 per cent of new supply will be from single-user factory spaces. Warehouse spaces will account for 29 per cent of the supply, while multiple-user factory space will account for 16 per cent. The remaining 2 per cent will come from business park space.ГӮВ 

In 2027 and 2028, an additional 2.5 million sq m of industrial space is expected to be completed. This translates to an average annual supply of about 1.1 million sq m from 2026 to 2028.

In comparison, the average annual supply and demand for industrial space were about 800,000 sq m and 600,000 sq m over the past three years, respectively.

data.title: AirAsia X targets up to US$600 million debt restructuring after combining airlines
data.description:
[KUALA LUMPUR] MalaysiaвҖҷs AirAsia X targets US$500 million to US$600 million in debt restructuring after the budget airline this week bought the short-haul aviation business of Capital A, deputy group CEO Farouk Kamal said.

AirAsia X, the medium-haul affiliate of Capital AвҖҷs AirAsia, plans to combine the firmsвҖҷ seven airlines under one banner.

вҖңWe are also looking at a lot of refinancing initiatives ... to stretch out the tenure, to reduce the interest costs and to collapse all our debt instruments into one or two loan instruments,вҖқ Farouk said in an interview on Wednesday (Jan 21).

The AirAsia group has become one of AsiaвҖҷs largest budget airline operators since its 2001 founding. However, pandemic travel restrictions decimated AirAsia parent Capital A prompting MalaysiaвҖҷs stock exchange to classify it as financially distressed.

Consolidation of all AirAsia-branded aviation businesses under AirAsia X will allow the airlines to focus on expanding operations and reducing costs, while Capital A focuses on reviving its finances.

AirAsia X plans flights to London from as early as the middle of this year, Farouk also said, more than a decade after it last operated flights to the British capitalвҖҷs Gatwick and Stansted airports. It launched flights to Istanbul in November.

The airline will also develop a hub in Bahrain to improve connectivity to Central Asia, the Middle East, Europe and Africa, Farouk said.

AirAsia X expects to receive delivery of four Airbus long-range A321LR aircraft this year which will enable it to expand beyond Asia, Farouk also said.

The airline, which has a 255-strong fleet, in July ordered 50 longer-range A321XLR aircraft and agreed the right to convert 20 models already on order to the single-aisle jet. It is also working with planemakers for regional-type aircraft, Farouk said, as it considers ordering a further 150 jets.

Following consolidation, AirAsia X targets near-term revenue approaching US$6 billion as well as an earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (Ebitda) margin of 20 per cent and a passenger load of over 80 per cent, chief financial officer Low Kar Chuan said in the same interview.

Low said the airline aims to repay all bank loans granted during the Covid-19 pandemic, when governmentsвҖҷ travel restrictions decimated income, within two to three years. REUTERS
